title: AgentMinds Reporting Profile (ARP) v1.2.1
description: Wire format for agent reports, warnings, learned patterns, recommendations and skill manifests. See docs/AGENT_REPORTING_PROFILE.md. ARP is a profile built on Sentry + OTel GenAI + MCP + Claude Skills + AGNTCY OASF; it is NOT a competing standard.

arp_version.description: Semver of the ARP profile the sender targets. Defaults to latest minor of same major.

ars_version.description: Legacy alias for arp_version (pre-rename). Accepted for backwards compatibility; prefer arp_version on new payloads.

agent.description: Stable agent name. Lowercase, snake/kebab case.

site_id.description: Stable site identifier. May be set by the transport layer if absent in body.

session_id.description: Optional session/run identifier — correlates a sequence of agent invocations within one logical workflow. Compatible with LangSmith Threads and OpenInference session.id semantics.

user_id.description: Optional opaque user identifier (NOT PII — collectors should hash or pseudonymise upstream). Maps to OpenInference user.id.

conversation_id.description: Optional conversation/thread identifier. Alias of OTel gen_ai.conversation.id; multi-turn agent flows MAY use this to group reports.

_meta.description: ARP §2.1 (v1.1): vendor extension envelope using reverse-DNS namespacing (e.g. com.agentminds.*, io.agentminds.*). MCP-aligned. Open by design; collectors MUST ignore unknown keys. Reserved second-label namespaces: agentminds.*, arp.*.

lifecycle_event.description: ARP §2.2 (v1.2): OPTIONAL signal for hibernate-aware runtimes (Cloudflare Agents Durable Object, AWS Lambda cold start, etc.). Affects warning interpretation — fresh wake-up warnings carry less weight than steady-state ones. MUST NOT be required for non-hibernating agents.

Fingerprint.description: ARP fingerprint identity. Either a single lowercase hex SHA-256 string (canonical ARP form), or an array of strings with optional `{{ default }}` placeholder (Sentry-aligned form, v1.1+). Collectors MUST accept either; producers SHOULD pick one form per payload.

oneOf.description: Canonical ARP — lowercase hex SHA-256 (64 chars).
Sentry-aligned array form — segments concatenated server-side. May include the literal `{{ default }}` placeholder at any position to inherit the SDK's default fingerprint.

Score.description: ARP §3.5 (v1.1): evaluation score primitive. Lineage: Langfuse Score model + LangSmith Feedback. Lets collectors carry NUMERIC / CATEGORICAL / BOOLEAN evaluation outcomes attached to warnings, patterns, or runs without overloading Recommendation.confidence.

data_type.description: Closed enum — keep this small, no vendor extensions.

value.description: Number for data_type=numeric, string for categorical, boolean for boolean.

compared_to_baseline_id.description: Optional baseline run identifier for comparative scoring.

Exception.description: ARP §3.7 (v1.2): structured exception payload. Sentry-aligned. Goes inside Warning.evidence.exception when an agent reports a thrown/caught error. Distinguishes handled vs uncaught for triage.

handled.description: True = caught by agent's try/except. False = uncaught (reached the top of the stack).

type.description: How the exception entered the report: 'exception_handler' / 'signal' / 'unhandled' / etc.

Breadcrumb.description: ARP §3.6 (v1.1.1): timestamped event leading up to a Warning. Sentry-aligned. Critical for understanding cascade failures.

data.description: Free-form structured data attached to the breadcrumb.

score.description: Optional 0-100 health score for the agent run.

scores.description: ARP §3.5 (v1.1): evaluation scores attached to this report. Use Score for typed eval outcomes; the legacy `report.score` 0-100 health number is for one-off summaries only.

handoffs.description: ARP §6.2 (v1.2): multi-agent delegation events that occurred during this report. OpenAI Agents SDK Handoff lineage.

Handoff.description: ARP §6.2 (v1.2): one multi-agent delegation event. Lineage: OpenAI Agents SDK `Handoff` class.

input_filter.description: Optional regex/spec describing how the input is filtered before delegation.

PromptManifest.description: ARP §6.3 (v1.2): prompt provenance. Lineage: OpenInference prompt.* attributes + LangSmith Prompt Hub.

template.description: Inline template body. Senders SHOULD provide either url OR template.

breadcrumbs.description: ARP §3.6 (v1.1.1): timestamped events leading up to this warning. Empty/omitted = no trail. Cap at ~50 items to avoid payload bloat.

Telemetry.description: ARP §5 Telemetry. Normative references (v1.1.1): OpenTelemetry GenAI semconv (gen_ai.* attribute namespace) and OpenInference v1 spec (openinference.span.kind classification, llm.* prompt/response attributes, retrieval/embedding/agent/tool conventions). Senders SHOULD use upstream attribute names verbatim; ARP-specific extensions go under the agentminds.* namespace per §2.1.

OpenInferenceSpanKind.description: OpenInference v1 canonical span kind taxonomy (10 values). Source: https://github.com/Arize-ai/openinference/blob/main/spec/semantic_conventions.md (Apache-2.0 spec text only; the Phoenix implementation is ELv2 and is NOT a dependency).

OpenAISpanSubtype.description: OpenAI Agents SDK 8-value span subtype enum. Adopted verbatim for cross-vendor span classification.

TelemetrySpan.description: ARP §5.1 (v1.1.1): typed telemetry span. Backwards-compat with v1.0 free-form `{type:object}` spans — all fields below are OPTIONAL.

dotted_order.description: ARP §5.3 (v1.2): LangSmith-compatible single-string hierarchical sort key (`<RFC3339>Z<uuid>.<RFC3339>Z<uuid>...`). Lets clients sort spans without a parent_id JOIN. OPTIONAL.

attributes.description: OTel + OpenInference attribute bag. Recommended namespaces: gen_ai.*, openinference.*, agentminds.*.

prompts.description: ARP §6.3 (v1.2): prompts in use by this project. Lets recommendation engines avoid telling you to add a prompt registry you already have.

tech_stack.description: ARP §6 tech stack. v1.2 adds MCP role disambiguation: `mcp_server_exposed` (bool — does this site expose tools?), `mcp_clients_consumed` (array of upstream MCP servers it consumes).

SkillManifest.description: ARP §6.1 SkillManifest — aligned with the agentskills.io open spec frontmatter (name, description, version, license, allowed_tools, compatibility, metadata). v1.1.1 adds the polarity-flipped auto_invocable flag (replacing Anthropic Skills' disable-model-invocation) so the default is the safer 'opt-in to auto-invocation'.

license.description: SPDX license identifier (e.g. 'MIT', 'Apache-2.0', 'CC-BY-4.0'). Aligned with agentskills.io.

auto_invocable.description: ARP §6.1 (v1.1.1): polarity-flipped from Anthropic Skills' `disable-model-invocation`. True = the LLM may load this skill without explicit user mention. Default false (opt-in) — safer.

metadata.description: agentskills.io open frontmatter `metadata` field — free-form key/value bag for skill-specific config (e.g. tags, owner, dashboard URLs).

oasf_skill_ids.description: ARP §6.1 (v1.2): cross-reference to AGNTCY OASF skill taxonomy IDs (e.g. 1106 for 'Cross-Site Pattern Confidence' under Category 11). Stable numeric refs let LF AI & Data tooling discover this skill via standard taxonomy.

agentskills_io_canonical_name.description: ARP §6.1 (v1.2): optional cross-link to canonical name on agentskills.io marketplace.

execution_tier.description: ARP §6.1 (v1.2): how this skill executes. Cloudflare Agents 5-tier ladder + ARP `in_process` extension. OPTIONAL.
